shared.validation_domain_needs_dot: Must contain at least one dot

traffic_engine.page_description: One DPI engine, two modes. Bypass carrier video throttling, or make HTTPS traffic look like a whitelisted service. Only one mode runs at a time.
traffic_engine.toast_video_enabled: Video Optimizer is now protecting video
traffic_engine.error_load_failed: Could not load the Traffic Engine from the device.
traffic_engine.alert_kernel_module_missing: Required kernel module not found. Install with opkg install kmod-nft-queue on the device.
traffic_engine.takeover_desc: The engine runs one mode at a time. Enabling {{enabling}} stops {{stopping}}. Applies instantly without a reboot.
traffic_engine.onboarding_description: The nfqws engine is not installed on this device yet.
traffic_engine.onboarding_unlocks: One install unlocks both Video Optimizer and Masquerade.
traffic_engine.advanced_remove_desc: Uninstall the nfqws binary from this device.
traffic_engine.advanced_dialog_desc: The nfqws binary is shared by both modes, so removing it disables Video Optimizer and Masquerade. You can reinstall it later from this page.
traffic_engine.advanced_toast_error: Failed to remove the engine binary
traffic_engine.status_card_description: What the packet engine is doing right now.
traffic_engine.video_tagline: Smoother streaming. Counters carrier throttling of video so it buffers less.
traffic_engine.masquerade_tagline: Hide your traffic. Disguises HTTPS under one whitelisted domain so the carrier can't single it out to throttle.
traffic_engine.bypass_helper_idle: Pins TTL and hop-limit to 64 so tethered devices are not flagged as a hotspot.
traffic_engine.bypass_helper_active: Active — all traffic leaves at TTL/HL 64.
traffic_engine.bypass_helper_external: Set elsewhere to TTL {{ttl}} / HL {{hl}} (TTL page or a SIM profile). Manage it there.
traffic_engine.bypass_toast_enabled: Hotspot bypass on — TTL/HL set to 64

ethernet.page_description: View detailed information about your device's Ethernet status, including connection status, speed, and other relevant metrics.
ethernet.card_description: Current Ethernet link status, speed, and duplex settings.
ethernet.error_load_failed: Could not load ethernet status from the device.
ethernet.toast_error_set_speed: Failed to set link speed limit

lan_config.card_description: Set the router's LAN IP address (the gateway your devices use) and the subnet size for the br-lan network.
lan_config.help_gateway: The router's own address on the LAN. Devices use it as their default gateway.
lan_config.help_subnet: Choose the size of the LAN network as a CIDR prefix.
lan_config.confirm_body: The LAN will switch to {{address}} (netmask {{netmask}}). This rebinds the network and your current connection will drop.
lan_config.confirm_reconnect: After applying, the network rebinds and your connection drops briefly. A router or device using DHCP will reconnect automatically within a few seconds; one with a static IP must be moved to the new subnet manually. You'll then browse to the new address to continue.
lan_config.applied_body: The device is moving to {{address}}. Your connection will drop for about {{seconds}} seconds — reconnect your LAN or Ethernet cable, then open the new address:
lan_config.applied_body_auto: The device is moving to {{address}}. A router set to DHCP should reconnect on its own within about {{seconds}} seconds and pick up the new gateway. If it doesn't, reconnect your cable, then open the new address:
lan_config.error_invalid_ipaddr: Enter a valid unicast IPv4 address (e.g. 192.168.1.1).
lan_config.error_invalid_host_in_subnet: This is the network or broadcast address for the chosen subnet. Pick a usable host address.
lan_config.error_invalid_prefix: Choose a subnet prefix between /16 and /30.

dns.page_description: Override carrier-assigned DNS servers with your preferred resolvers for all devices on the local network.
dns.card_description: Override carrier DNS servers for all devices on your local network.
dns.error_at_least_one: Enter at least one DNS server address
dns.toast_error_apply: Failed to apply DNS settings — check connection

ttl_mtu.page_description: Set custom Time To Live, Hop Limit, and Maximum Transmission Unit values on the cellular data interface.

ttl.card_description: Set custom TTL (IPv4) and Hop Limit (IPv6) values applied to outbound packets on the cellular interface.
ttl.alert_profile_override: TTL/HL configuration is managed by the {{profileName}} Custom SIM Profile.
ttl.error_at_least_one: Enter at least a TTL or Hop Limit value
ttl.toast_error_apply: Failed to apply TTL/Hop Limit settings

mtu.card_description: Set the maximum packet size on the cellular data interface. Lower values can help with fragmentation issues.

ippt.page_description: Assign the modem's public IP directly to a connected device, bypassing the router's NAT layer.
ippt.card_description: Assign the modem's public IP directly to a downstream device, bypassing the router's NAT.
ippt.helper_mac_manual: Enter the MAC address of the device that will receive the WAN IP.
ippt.toast_error_apply: Failed to save IP Passthrough settings
ippt.dialog_desc_reboot_notice: Applying these changes will save the configuration and immediately reboot the device.
ippt.dialog_desc_unreachable_warning: Once IP Passthrough is active, the device's local gateway will no longer be reachable. Make sure you have an active Tailscale connection or another out-of-band method to access the device after reboot.

locked_by_verizon.title: IP Passthrough is managed by your Verizon profile
locked_by_verizon.body: Active profile: "{{name}}". To change these settings, deactivate the profile from Custom Profiles.

masquerade.page_description: Make all HTTPS traffic appear as a whitelisted service to carrier DPI
masquerade.card_description: Make HTTPS traffic appear as a whitelisted service to carrier DPI.
masquerade.card_description_full: Make HTTPS traffic appear as a whitelisted service to carrier DPI by injecting fake TLS handshakes with a spoofed domain. Some carriers may detect this behavior and de-prioritize your connection.
masquerade.error_binary_not_installed: The `nfqws` binary is not installed on this device.
masquerade.error_install_from_video_optimizer: Install it from the <link>Video Optimizer</link> page, then check again.
masquerade.alert_video_optimizer_active: Video Optimizer is currently active. <link>Disable it</link> before enabling Traffic Masquerade.
masquerade.shared_engine_note: Shares one DPI engine with <link>Video Optimizer</link>. Only one can run at a time.
masquerade.toggle_reassurance: Applies instantly without rebooting. Turn it off anytime to revert.
masquerade.alert_kernel_module_missing: Required kernel module not found. Install with opkg install kmod-nft-queue on the device.
masquerade.helper_domain: The domain that appears in the fake TLS handshake sent to the carrier.
masquerade.help_domain: Carriers read the SNI — the server name sent unencrypted in every TLS handshake — to decide what to throttle. Masquerade rewrites it to this domain so your traffic looks like that service.
masquerade.aria_domain_info: More information about the masquerade domain
masquerade.test_hint: A pass means your HTTPS handshakes are carrying the spoofed name, so the carrier sees this domain instead of the real one.
masquerade.aria_test_hint: What a passing injection test means
masquerade.section_remove_binary_desc: Uninstall the nfqws binary from this device.
masquerade.dialog_uninstall_desc: This will remove the nfqws binary and disable both Video Optimizer and Traffic Masquerade. You can reinstall it from the Video Optimizer page.
masquerade.dialog_uninstall_other_active: Video Optimizer is currently active and will be turned off.
masquerade.test_description: Confirm fake SNI packets are being injected into HTTPS traffic

video_optimizer.page_description: Bypass carrier video throttling using DPI evasion on targeted video CDN hostnames
video_optimizer.card_description: Bypass carrier video throttling on cellular connections using DPI evasion.
video_optimizer.card_description_full: Bypass carrier video throttling on cellular connections using DPI evasion. Some carriers (e.g., T-Mobile) may detect DPI evasion and de-prioritize your connection.
video_optimizer.error_binary_not_installed: The `nfqws` binary is not installed on this device.
video_optimizer.error_download_zapret: Download it automatically from the <link>zapret</link> project.
video_optimizer.alert_masquerade_active: Traffic Masquerade is currently active. <link>Disable it</link> before enabling Video Optimizer.
video_optimizer.shared_engine_note: Shares one DPI engine with <link>Traffic Masquerade</link>. Only one can run at a time.
video_optimizer.toggle_reassurance: Applies instantly without rebooting. Turn it off anytime to revert.
video_optimizer.alert_kernel_module_missing: Required kernel module not found. Install with opkg install kmod-nft-queue on the device.
video_optimizer.help_desync_repeats: Number of times the DPI-evading packet is sent for each matched flow. Higher values improve reliability when packets are lost or reordered, but increase CPU use and upstream bandwidth. Default 1 works for most users.
video_optimizer.helper_desync_repeats: Higher values resist stricter DPI but add a little overhead. 1 suits most networks.
video_optimizer.verify_hint: A pass means the engine is intercepting traffic to your hostlisted domains and applying DPI evasion.
video_optimizer.section_remove_binary_desc: Uninstall the nfqws binary from this device.
video_optimizer.dialog_uninstall_desc: This will remove the nfqws binary and disable both Video Optimizer and Traffic Masquerade. You can reinstall it later from this page.
video_optimizer.dialog_uninstall_other_active: Traffic Masquerade is currently active and will be turned off.
video_optimizer.verify_description: Confirm DPI evasion is intercepting traffic for hostlisted domains
video_optimizer.hostlist_card_description: Hostnames targeted for DPI evasion — video CDN domains that will have their TLS handshakes modified
video_optimizer.toast_import_no_new: No new domains to import — all already in list
video_optimizer.dialog_restore_desc: This will replace the current list with the factory default CDN hostnames. Any custom domains you added will be removed.
